title: caucalis leptophylla subsp. elongata
intro: Caucalis leptophylla subsp. elongata, also known as the White Horehound or Long-leaved Wild Horehound, is a perennial plant species in the family Apiaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, making it useful in treating respiratory issues such as coughs and congestion. The plant is rich in flavonoids, which have been shown to have antioxidant and antimicrobial effects, contributing to its health benefits, including reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. Caucalis leptophylla subsp. elongata can be prepared as an herbal tea or infused oil, and is sometimes used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
